altitude.transcript: Within the first few days above 1,500 meters, the volume of plasma in your blood decreases, which increases the concentration of hemoglobin. Over the next two weeks your hemoglobin levels will continue to rise, allowing your blood to carry even more oxygen per milliliter.
altitude.summary: The body adapts to high altitudes by decreasing blood plasma volume and increasing hemoglobin levels, which ultimately enhances the blood's ability to transport oxygen.

calm.transcript: But why does being nervous undermine expert performance? There are two sets of theories, which both say that primarily, choking under pressure boils down to focus. First, there are the distraction theories. These suggest that performance suffers when the mind is preoccupied with worries, doubt
calm.summary: Expert performance often declines under pressure due to a loss of focus. Distraction theories explain this by suggesting that performance suffers when the mind becomes preoccupied with worries and doubts.

depression.transcript: As a Black woman, I've had to develop extraordinary resilience to succeed. And like most people in my community, I had the misconception that depression was a sign of weakness, a character flaw. But I wasn't weak. I was a high achiever. I'd earned a master's degree in media studies and had a string of high-profile jobs in the film and television industries.
depression.summary: A highly successful woman reflects on her journey and challenges the misconception within her community that depression is a sign of weakness. She highlights her resilience and professional achievements to prove that mental health struggles do not equate to a character flaw.

happiness.transcript: First up, crowdsourcing my 420 sq ft apartment in Manhattan with partners mutopo and jovoto.com. I wanted it all. Home office, sit-down dinner for 10, room for guests, and all my kite surfing gear. With over 300 entries from around the world, I got it. My own little jewel box.
happiness.summary: The speaker successfully crowdsourced the design of a small Manhattan apartment to accommodate multiple functions, resulting in a highly customized and efficient living space.

healthy.transcript: The majority, 60 percent, yielded to sexual temptations within one year, and most of them did so without using birth control. So much for promises. Now let's tempt four-year-olds, giving them a treat. They can have one marshmallow now, but if they wait until the experimenter comes back, they can have two. Of course it pays, if you like marshmallows, to wait.
healthy.summary: The speaker contrasts two examples of yielding to temptation: teenagers breaking sexual abstinence pledges and four-year-olds participating in the marshmallow test, illustrating different aspects of self-control.

menopause.transcript: So in terms of a timeline, most women go through menopause in their early 50s. But it can be earlier, often because of medical interventions. And the common example is a hysterectomy and/or an oophorectomy, which is the surgical removal of the uterus and/or the ovaries.
menopause.summary: While menopause typically occurs in a woman's early 50s, medical procedures like hysterectomies or oophorectomies can induce it earlier.

speak.transcript: Seven deadly sins of speaking. I'm not pretending this is an exhaustive list, but these seven, I think, are pretty large habits that we can all fall into. First, gossip. Speaking ill of somebody who's not present. Not a nice habit, and we know perfectly well the person gossiping, five minutes later, will be gossiping
speak.summary: The speaker introduces the "seven deadly sins of speaking," starting with gossip, which is defined as speaking negatively about someone who isn't present, and notes its pervasive nature.

thanks.transcript: that I would want to say thank you to someone, I would want to praise them, I would want to take in their praise of me and I'd just stop it. And I asked myself, why? I felt shy, I felt embarrassed. And then my question became, am I the only one who does this? So I decided to investigate.
thanks.summary: The speaker reflects on their personal habit of withholding praise and deflecting compliments due to shyness and embarrassment, leading them to investigate if others share this behavior.
